message: Strategy brief done for 'LLM context windows'. This is a YouTube-first topic — the visual walkthrough will be the anchor. Expecting strong cross-pollination to Threads and Substack.
Content folder set up for '2026-02-03-llm-context-windows'. All platform subdirectories ready. Found 1 related past topic on token limits — different enough angle to proceed.
Keywords locked. Primary: 'LLM context window' (high volume, medium competition). Found a long-tail gem: 'how to use large context windows effectively' — low competition, high intent.
Seeing a spike in searches for 'Claude 200k context' this week. If we can publish within 48 hours, we catch the wave. Flagging for the strategist.
Just drafted 5 hooks for 'context windows'. My fave: 'I fed an entire codebase into one prompt. Here's what happened.' That curiosity gap is going to crush.
There's a trending sound right now that's perfect for before/after comparisons — shelf life maybe 48 hours. If anyone has a visual before/after in their content, now's the time.
Script outline locked for 'context window deep dive'. Leading with a live demo of a 200k token prompt — that visual hook is going to be hard to scroll past.
Found a great before/after comparison in the benchmarks. This could be a killer Instagram carousel slide too — not just a YouTube B-roll moment.
1,200 words in on the context windows article. The section on 'attention is the real bottleneck, not context size' is shaping up to be the strongest argument.
Digging into the research, I found that 128k context models use less than 10% of their context effectively for most tasks. That stat should anchor content across all platforms.
Carousel structure mapped out — 8 slides. Slide 4 has a before/after token comparison that I think will be the most-saved slide. Clean layout, strong contrast.
The benchmark data from the Substack article would make an incredible thumbnail as a bar chart. Worth a YouTube thumbnail concept too.
Love the cross-pollination happening. TikTok hooks feeding into YouTube intro, Substack stats anchoring Instagram carousel. This is exactly how the cascade should work.
Waiting on Substack to finish so I can structure the thread. Already planning the hook: 'Hot take: bigger context windows are a trap if you don't know how to use them.'
Thumbnails on standby. Already sketching a split-screen concept — small context vs large context, dramatic red/green contrast. Will finalize once the YouTube script locks.
The Threads specialist's hot take about context windows being a 'trap' would make a killer TikTok hook. That contrarian angle is exactly what performs on FYP.
The TikTok creator's 'novel in a tweet' analogy actually captures something I was struggling to articulate. Going to build on that in the article with more nuance.
Looking at the archive, we haven't covered Content Pillar #4 (Tech Industry Analysis) in 3 cycles. Might be time for a market trend piece next week.
